stories.headline: SEC-CFTC joint guidance puts BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, LINK in 'digital commodity' bucket
stories.tldr: A landmark joint interpretation between the SEC and CFTC sorts crypto assets into five legal categories, ending years of jurisdictional limbo.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Naming BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, and LINK as digital commodities hands the CFTC primary oversight of the largest tokens and clears a path for spot products built around them.
the_details: The framework defines five buckets: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins, and digital securities.
CFTC commits to administering the Commodity Exchange Act consistent with the SEC interpretation, ending overlapping enforcement risk.
Coalition of 100+ crypto firms is now pushing the Senate Banking Committee to mark up the Clarity Act on the same framework.

stories.headline: Spot bitcoin ETFs string together 8-day, $2.1B inflow run as BTC reclaims $77K
stories.tldr: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs absorbed $2.1 billion across eight straight sessions through April 23, dragging cumulative net inflows since launch to $58 billion.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Institutional demand has turned the year's ETF flows positive even while short-term holders distribute, creating the first sustained structural bid since February.
the_details: April monthly inflows hit $2.43 billion, fully erasing Q1 outflows; total ETF assets now sit at $102 billion.
BTC trades at $77,160 on Wednesday after a 13% April rally, with Ethereum at $2,330.
Capital is rotating into ETH funds, which logged a record $11.68 billion cumulative inflow.

stories.headline: Consensys, Joseph Lubin pledge up to 30,000 ETH to rsETH bridge-exploit recovery
stories.tldr: Consensys and founder Joseph Lubin are committing up to 30,000 ETH to DeFi United's rescue effort for Kelp DAO users hit by the April 18 LayerZero bridge exploit.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A name-brand backstop turns the patchy Aave-led rescue into a credible industry-wide standard for handling cross-chain failures.
the_details: Kelp DAO lost roughly $293 million on April 18 after an attacker minted 116,500 unbacked rsETH and borrowed against it on Aave.
Aave's TVL dropped from $26.4B to about $20B in the hours after the exploit, prompting the coordinated rescue.
Lido, EtherFi, and other LST issuers had already committed parts of the $300M industry pledge before the Consensys add-on.

stories.headline: IRS digital-asset cost-basis reporting now mandatory for every U.S. crypto broker
stories.tldr: From April 15, every U.S. digital-asset broker must report customer cost basis to the IRS, ending the wallet-tracking grace period traders have enjoyed since 2022.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Mandatory reporting closes the largest crypto tax loophole and sets up automated audits on every centralized exchange withdrawal starting next filing season.
the_details: Rule applies to centralized exchanges, custodial wallets, and payment processors handling over $10K in annual digital-asset volume.
FinCEN and OFAC also rolled out parallel AML rules for primary providers of stablecoin infrastructure on April 8.
FDIC and Treasury proposed companion stablecoin issuer and 'substantially similar' state-regime rules earlier in April.
stories.source: Latham & Watkins US Crypto Policy Tracker

stories.headline: Japan's Bitbank rolls out crypto credit card that pays bills directly in BTC
stories.tldr: Japanese exchange Bitbank launched a credit card letting users settle monthly bills from their on-exchange crypto holdings, with 0.5% cashback paid in BTC.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Direct exchange-to-merchant rails in a G7 jurisdiction normalize crypto as a spending account, not just an investment, and pressure card networks to follow.
the_details: Bills are deducted from the user's Bitbank balance and converted at the time of statement settlement.
Cashback is paid back into BTC on the 1st of the month at a flat 0.5% on all qualifying spend.
Launch follows Japan's 2026 FSA reclassification of crypto as a financial product, easing custodial card-network partnerships.
